title: EPA Releases Proposal for Commercial Sterilizers to Safeguard the Supply of Life-Saving Medical Tools
claim_supported: EPA proposes to reconsider/relax the 2024 EtO commercial-sterilizer NESHAP rule, arguing the 2024 rule exceeded CAA Sec.112(f)(2) authority; frames EtO as sterilizing 50% of US medical devices with no ready alternative; 45-day comment period.
locator: Press-release body, 'Overview' and 'Stated Rationale' sections, fetched directly this session
notes: Directly refreshes the seed's `launch_2030_timing_thesis` assumption #1 (durable EtO regulatory pressure), which this proposal directly contradicts as of March 2026. Load-bearing for D-08 G1 verdict.

india_origin_audit.notes: Official US federal regulator domain (epa.gov). No India connection.

title: EPA Extends Comment Period on Proposed Revisions to Ethylene Oxide Standards
authors_or_org: U.S. Small Business Administration, Office of Advocacy
claim_supported: Comment period on EPA's EtO reconsideration extended to May 15, 2026; details three specific relaxations (aeration-vent standards, monitoring-method choice, PTE rescission); estimates cost savings of approximately $43-48 million/year through 2046.
locator: Page body, comment-period and cost-savings paragraphs, fetched directly this session
notes: Quantifies the regulatory-rollback direction and magnitude; used to temper the seed's 'durable regulatory forcing function' framing. TIER UPGRADE (P4 curation): retiered T2->T1 -- this is an official US federal agency (SBA Office of Advocacy) primary economic-impact record on a .gov domain, meeting the T1 definition of a government primary record.

institutions.name: U.S. Small Business Administration, Office of Advocacy

india_origin_audit.notes: Official US federal government domain (advocacy.sba.gov). No India connection.

title: First quarter 2026 update: starting the year on track, with product innovation progress
claim_supported: IBA Q1 2026: consolidated backlog EUR1.6B (equipment EUR0.73B, services EUR0.83B); Industrial Solutions segment book-to-bill 0.8x on a 2-year rolling basis (equipment backlog EUR218M); launched Rhodotron LITE low-power X-ray accelerator for small irradiation centres; increased conference presence in US and China; PFAS-treatment application progressing toward an industrial competitive solution.
locator: Press-release body, Industrial Solutions and backlog sections, fetched directly this session
notes: Refreshes atlas L05-028's FY2025 figures with a Q1 2026 update; book-to-bill below 1.0x tempers the 'backlogged for years' framing in the seed.

india_origin_audit.notes: IBA is headquartered in Louvain-la-Neuve, Belgium. No India connection.

title: Sotera Health Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
claim_supported: CFO Jonathan Lyons: FY2026 capex guidance $175-225M. CEO Michael Petras: new X-ray-modality Sterigenics facility online later in 2026, another late 2027/early 2028; ~$200M already spent on EO facility enhancements; company proceeding with upgrades 'as if the rule that's in place is gonna be the requirement,' i.e. independent of the EPA reconsideration outcome.
locator: Earnings-call transcript, capital-expenditure and regulatory-outlook Q&A sections, fetched directly this session
notes: Named, dated, dollar-denominated primary buyer/procurement demand evidence -- the strongest single demand data point found for D-08.

india_origin_audit.notes: Sotera Health Company (parent of Sterigenics) is headquartered in Broadview Heights, Ohio, US; NASDAQ-listed. No India connection.

title: NIH/NCI 461 -- Ultra-Fast Dose Rate (FLASH) Radiation Detectors and Safety Systems for Cancer Treatment (SBIR Contract Topic)
authors_or_org: National Cancer Institute (NIH/NCI) SBIR Development Center
claim_supported: NCI SBIR contract topic (PHS-2023-1 cycle) sought FLASH radiation detectors/safety systems spanning 2-10 Gy/min to 40-120 Gy/s dose rates, <5% dose-monitoring tolerance, mandatory NIST traceability; Phase I up to $400K/12mo, Phase II up to $2M/2yr, 2-3 anticipated awards; deadline Nov 14, 2023 (expired instance, establishes a recurring federal contract vehicle for this exact capability).
locator: Solicitation page body, funding/deadline/technical-requirements sections, fetched directly this session
notes: A federal CONTRACT vehicle (not a grant) for exactly the instrument category D-09 proposes; strongest available primary buyer evidence for this idea. This specific instance is expired; a verifier should check for a live PHS-2026/2027 recurrence.

india_origin_audit.notes: US federal government agency (NIH/NCI), official .gov-affiliated domain. No India connection.

title: flashDiamond Detector (product page)
claim_supported: PTW's flashDiamond UHDR detector, developed with University of Rome 'Tor Vergata', is in use at 12+ European labs/hospitals but is explicitly labeled 'for research use only'; tested for relative dosimetry in UHDR conditions and is a 'promising candidate' (not yet confirmed) for reference dosimetry.
locator: Product page body, specifications and clinical-use sections, fetched directly this session
notes: Closest named incumbent competitor for D-09; confirms the FLASH-dosimetry field is real but still at a pre-commercial/research-tool stage, corroborating a small near-term installed base.

india_origin_audit.notes: PTW Dosimetry (PTW-Freiburg GmbH) is headquartered in Freiburg, Germany. No India connection.

title: EXCLUSIVE: US Military Awards Joint Laser Weapon System Contracts
claim_supported: US Navy plans to award $31.7M in contracts for Joint Beam Control System development as soon as Q4 2026, plus $30M for procurement/testing of containerized hardware by March 2027; Army+Navy JLWS R&D spending totals $675.93M through FY2031 ($337.8M Army FY2028-31, $243.3M Navy); references nLIGHT's prior coherent-beam-combining (CBC) work for HELSI (2019) as the technical lineage for JLWS beam control.
locator: Article body, Navy beam-control contract and JLWS R&D total sections, fetched directly this session
notes: Names a specific, dated, dollar-denominated beam-control (not just laser-weapon) procurement line item -- the single strongest demand data point found across all five assigned ideas. Reports on official DoD budget-justification documents not independently re-fetched in primary PDF form this session.

india_origin_audit.notes: US defense-industry trade newsletter covering US DoD programs; no India connection identified.

title: 레이저대공무기 '천광' 핵심부품 국산화...1~2초 내 드론 격추 (Laser Anti-Air Weapon 'Cheongwang' Core Component Localized... Drone Interception Within 1-2 Seconds)
authors_or_org: Defense Acquisition Program Administration (DAPA, 방위사업청), Republic of Korea
claim_supported: Published June 1, 2026: DAPA announced successful localization of the laser oscillator for the 'Cheongwang' laser anti-air weapon (Block-1, operational since Dec 2024, described as world's first operational laser anti-air weapon); domestic oscillator delivers >=50% performance improvement over the previously-imported component; domestic content ratio (by budget) rose from 76% to 90%; drone engagement time reduced from 2-4s to 1-2s.
locator: Article body, program-overview and performance-improvement sections, fetched directly this session
notes: Korean local-language official government primary source satisfying the Asia local-language quota; establishes Korea as a fielded (not just prototype) side-market laser-weapons competitor/context, though CBC use specifically is not confirmed in this source.

institutions.name: Defense Acquisition Program Administration (DAPA), Republic of Korea

india_origin_audit.notes: Official Republic of Korea government domain (korea.kr, national policy briefing portal). No India connection.

title: Israel to mount lasers on fighter jets and helicopters
claim_supported: Elbit Systems developing airborne high-power laser weapons for Israeli Air Force fighter jets/helicopters, building on Rafael's ground-based Iron Beam system, which this article states operates at 10-100 kW; program traces to Israel's 'Epic Fury' joint operation with the US against Iran; no specific fielding timeline given.
locator: Article body, program-description and power-level sections, fetched directly this session
notes: Used as competitive/technical context (not a target market under the binding US/China geography rule); the 10-100kW figure conflicts with an unfetched nextbigfuture.com claim of a '100kW fully operational' Iron Beam -- conflict is preserved, not averaged, per mission rule 12.

india_origin_audit.notes: US-based defense trade publication (Sightline Media Group); subject matter concerns Israel, not India. No India connection.

title: 中国单兵激光武器亮相 便携反无人机利器 (Chinese Portable Laser Weapon Unveiled: Portable Anti-Drone Tool)
claim_supported: Harbin Xinguang Photoelectric's 'Lijian' (利剑) portable laser-weapon series: Lijian II/III ~2kW, ~500m range, ~25-30kg, ~4s drone-kill time; Lijian-10G (fixed) 10kW, 1,200m range; ~CNY 2 million/unit; deployed at some domestic military airports.
locator: Article body, specifications and deployment sections, fetched directly this session
notes: China-side laser-weapon competitor context; confirms fielded Chinese laser weapons found this session are a materially lower (kW-class) power tier than JLWS's 150-500kW target, and gives no indication of coherent beam combining specifically.

india_origin_audit.notes: China-based media outlet reporting on a China-based manufacturer (Harbin Xinguang Photoelectric). No India connection.

title: ASML 1000W light source to deliver 330wph EUV processing by 2030
claim_supported: ASML developed a 1000W EUV light source (doubling tin-droplet rate to 100,000/s with two-pulse shaping vs. single pulse) to raise High-NA throughput from 220 to 330 wph by 2030, using its existing CO2-driven architecture; ASML's lead EUV-source technologist Michael Purvis states a 'reasonably clear path toward 1,500 watts, and no fundamental reason why we couldn't get to 2,000 watts,' with no mention of switching drive wavelength.
locator: Article body, power-roadmap and technologist-quote sections, fetched directly this session
notes: Directly contradicts the seed's demand_trigger framing that EUV power-roadmap pressure creates urgency for a 2-micron drive-laser switch; load-bearing for the D-11 G1 FAIL verdict on the demand leg.

india_origin_audit.notes: UK-based electronics trade publication reporting on Dutch company ASML. No India connection.

title: LLNL Selected to Lead Next-Gen Extreme Ultraviolet Lithography Research
claim_supported: DOE Office of Science Microelectronics Science Research Centers (MSRC, CHIPS and Science Act) program: LLNL leads a 4-year, $12M project (within $179M for three MSRCs) testing whether the petawatt-class BAT laser (thulium-doped YLF, ~2-micron) can raise EUV source efficiency ~10x vs. CO2 lasers; named partners include ASML San Diego, SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory, and ARCNL (Netherlands).
locator: Article body, funding, research-focus, and partners sections, fetched directly this session
notes: The one genuine demand-adjacent primary source for D-11: a government-funded research hedge with the actual buyer (ASML San Diego) as a named technical partner -- but not a procurement commitment. Distinguished explicitly from a buyer tender in the evidence file.

india_origin_audit.notes: US Department of Energy national laboratory, official llnl.gov domain. No India connection.

title: Production of 13.5 nm light with 5% conversion efficiency from 2 um laser-driven tin microdroplet plasma
authors_or_org: Y. Mostafa; L. Behnke; D. J. Engels; Z. Bouza; J. Sheil; W. Ubachs; O. O. Versolato (Advanced Research Center for Nanolithography, ARCNL / Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam)
claim_supported: Peer-reviewed (Applied Physics Letters 123, 234101, 2023, with a 2024 erratum): demonstrates 5.0% in-band EUV conversion efficiency from a 2-micron-laser-driven, preshaped tin-microdroplet target -- exceeding the judge's CE>=4% gate on the raw number. Independently cross-referenced search sources indicate the 2-micron main pulse ran at 10 Hz repetition rate in this experiment, roughly 3-4 orders of magnitude below the ~50,000-100,000 Hz droplet/pulse rate an industrial kilowatt-class EUV source requires -- i.e. 'relevant rep-rate' is not demonstrated by this result.
locator: PDF retrieved directly (200 response) this session via ARCNL institutional repository; content confirmed to be the target paper via matching title/venue in search cross-reference; full text not machine-extractable from the binary PDF this session, so the 10 Hz rep-rate detail is corroborated via independent WebSearch synthesis rather than direct text extraction.
notes: Central to the honest evaluation of the judge-mandated CE>=4% physics gate for D-11: the CE number is met, the rep-rate qualifier is not. DOI not independently confirmed this session (AIP internal DOI not resolved via a fetched Crossref/AIP record); canonical_key uses the normalized institutional-repository URL per the source-standards fallback rule.

india_origin_audit.notes: All named authors resolve to ARCNL/Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam, Netherlands (a public-private research consortium including ASML as an industry partner). No India connection.

title: ASML Just Got Hit by a New China Export Threat. Is This a Buying Opportunity or a Red Flag?
claim_supported: ASML guided China to ~20% of 2026 revenue (down from 33% in 2025); the proposed MATCH Act would ban export of DUV immersion systems to China and ban servicing of already-installed Chinese equipment; ASML has never shipped an EUV machine to China; most analysts estimate China is at least a decade from independent EUV capability.
locator: Article body, China-revenue-guidance and MATCH-Act sections, fetched directly this session
notes: Establishes that D-11's product category (EUV drive-laser module) has no legal China market at all, now or through 2030-2034 -- decisive for the CN verdict.

india_origin_audit.notes: US-based financial media company. No India connection.

title: The Fluid That Made Two-Phase Immersion Cooling Work Just Became a Liability Worth $12.5 Billion
claim_supported: 3M announced (Dec 2022) exit from all PFAS manufacturing by end of 2025; last Novec orders accepted March 31, 2025; manufacturing lines shut down by end of 2025, leaving two-phase immersion cooling operators (built around Novec 7100/649) without a qualified fluid supply as of 2026. Replacement fluids named: Chemours Opteon 2P50 (HFO-based, GWP 10) and Opteon SF33 (ZutaCore's interim fluid); ZutaCore committed to a fully PFAS-free two-phase fluid 'by 2026.' The $12.5B figure refers to 3M's unrelated PFAS drinking-water litigation settlement (context, not a cooling-industry-specific figure).
locator: Article body, PFAS phase-out timeline and named-company sections, fetched directly this session
notes: Publisher identity/organization could not be independently confirmed this session (About page returned 404); no India-origin indicator was found in any fetched content (all-English, US-data-center-industry focus, sponsor contact in standard commercial form), and the domain is not among the known India-registered consultancies flagged in the mission brief (Mordor Intelligence, MarketsandMarkets, etc.). Treated as T3 trade press and corroborated against already-accepted atlas records (L14-033, L14-034, L14-046) rather than relied on alone for any load-bearing figure.

institutions.name: The Cooling Report (publisher identity not fully confirmed)

india_origin_audit.notes: Domain/content consistent with a US data-center-industry trade newsletter; no India-entity indicators found; not one of the flagged India-registered market-research consultancies. Confidence is moderate, not high, given the failed About-page fetch; used only as corroborating T3 context alongside independently-verified atlas sources.

title: Eaton's Boyd Thermal Acquisition ($9.5 Billion)
claim_supported: Eaton's $9.5B acquisition of Boyd's thermal division closed March 12, 2026; Boyd Thermal makes cold plates, CDUs, and thermal-interface manifold assemblies for direct-to-chip liquid cooling, with projected 2026 sales of $1.7B total / $1.5B liquid-cooling; three major liquid-cooling acquisitions totaling ~$19B closed within an 11-day window (including Ecolab/CoolIT at $4.75B); market described as shifting from specialty vendors to industrial-conglomerate consolidation.
locator: Article body, deal-value and market-consolidation sections, fetched directly this session
notes: Same publisher-verification caveat as P3R2-D-12-S01 applies. Used to establish that D-12's target customer base (cold-plate OEMs) is consolidating around large, well-capitalized incumbents -- directly relevant to why no named-OEM EHD co-development agreement (the judge's 2028 gate) has yet appeared.

institutions.name: The Cooling Report (publisher identity not fully confirmed)

india_origin_audit.notes: Same moderate-confidence publisher note as P3R2-D-12-S01; the named transacting companies (Eaton, Boyd) are unambiguously US-based, which independently supports non-India origin of the subject matter even though the publisher's own corporate registration was not fully confirmed.
